factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US Hormuz)
factors.summary: Fresh US military strikes on Iranian military site and drone interceptions near Strait of Hormuz escalate tensions despite ceasefire holding. Oil jumped to $89.35, raising inflation and cost-of-capital risks for NDX.

factors.name: Energy Inflation Persistence
factors.summary: Fed officials Goolsbee, Kashkari, Jefferson, and ECB's Lane all flagged persistent energy shock effects and second-round inflation risks. Kashkari stated inflation 'much too high' and labor market 'in decent shape' — supporting hawkish hold/hike bias.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Messaging
factors.summary: Multiple Fed speakers (Cook, Kashkari, Jefferson) signaled readiness to raise rates if inflation doesn't ease; Jefferson said monetary policy 'well positioned' but did not prejudge June. BoK also showed hawkish dissent (2 members voted for hike), hinting broader tightening cycle.

factors.name: AI Capex & Semiconductor Demand (Positive)
factors.summary: Snowflake surged 36% on $6B AWS commitment; Micron posting best monthly gain since 1987; Heico beat earnings. Australia data centre capex surged 6.5%. Hyperscaler infrastructure spending remains robust tailwind.

factors.name: Tech Earnings Mixed, Some Guidance Disappointments
factors.summary: Salesforce beat earnings but disappointed on revenue outlook (soft guidance); Zscaler crashed >31% on downbeat guidance; Marvell fell despite 'exceptional' AI demand. Sentiment skittish on software disruption fears.

factors.name: Morgan Stanley S&P 500 Target & Caution
factors.summary: MS raised SPX target to 8300 but warned rally is 'narrow,' household finances under strain, and long-term rate risk rising. Conflicting signals: bullish target but cautionary tone on breadth and sustainability.

factors.name: Long-Duration Growth Multiple Compression Risk
factors.summary: Energy shocks, hawkish Fed messaging, and escalating geopolitical risk raise real yields and nominal yields; Japanese 10Y JGB hit 2.72% amid fiscal concerns and BoJ hike speculation. Long-duration tech (NDX core) highly sensitive.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & FX Volatility
factors.summary: USD/JPY hit 4-week high ~159.60; USD/CAD near 1.3800 (highest since April 13); EUR/USD below 1.1650. Strong dollar headwind for NDX components with international revenue exposure.

factors.name: PCE Inflation Data Imminent
factors.summary: US PCE data looms in next 24-48h. Hot read would confirm persistence, cold read could ease inflation fears. This is the single highest-impact near-term catalyst for NQ repricing.

factors.name: Recent Price Action (4h/24h)
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.30% in 4h, +0.45% in 24h — very modest gains into record highs. Move is muted; not extended yet. Suggests market is consolidating or awaiting PCE data before aggressive re-rating.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught in a tension between robust AI/capex fundamentals and rising real-rate risks from persistent energy inflation and hawkish Fed messaging. Fresh Iran-US escalation at Hormuz has renewed oil volatility and geopolitical premium, pushing long-duration growth multiples under pressure. Modest overnight gains (+0.45% in 24h) suggest market is treading water ahead of critical PCE print. Earnings season is mixed: hyperscaler cloud capex remains a tailwind, but forward guidance disappointments (Salesforce, Zscaler) and caution from sell-side analysts (MS warning on narrow rally, rising household stress) are temperering enthusiasm.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE inflation data (May, next 24–48h) — hot print would confirm second-round effects, likely BEARISH for NDX
FOMC June meeting expectations (Fed speakers signaling June decision not pre-judged; market pricing hike odds)
Remaining mega-cap tech earnings and cloud capex guidance (AWS, Azure, GCP commentary critical)
Iran-US ceasefire negotiations update — any de-escalation would relieve oil/inflation premium
BoJ June rate decision signaling (June/July hike now priced at >50%; impacts global rates)
screening.risk_warning: PCE surprise hot (>0.4% m/m or >2.8% y/y) would trigger sharp re-rating of real yields and growth multiples, pushing NQ lower 1–3%. Conversely, cooler inflation and any Iran peace progress would relieve pressure and support rally continuation. Current phase is consolidation ahead of data; late entries chasing here expose to retest risk if PCE disappoints.
screening.entry_guidance: Consolidation likely until PCE prints; avoid chasing into record highs. If PCE cool, look for buy dips toward 29800–29900 support. If hot, expect retest lower; consider short on retest of intraday highs (30050–30150) with tight stops.

factors.name: Middle East escalation (US-Iran strikes)
factors.summary: Fresh US airstrikes on Iranian military targets near Hormuz Strait have triggered risk-off moves. Oil jumped 2.5-2.9% to $97/bbl (Brent), raising stagflation concerns and compressing growth multiples. Markets initially rallied to record highs before reversals post-strike news (per Deutsche Bank: 'equities have turned lower after fresh war shock').

factors.name: Semiconductor rally skepticism
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports the chip stock rally is 'the most hated in history' with traders heavily shorting (negative positioning). Bloomberg notes Micron's cheap valuation despite strong 2026 performance may be a 'warning sign' rather than bullish signal, suggesting overextension or exhaustion risk.

factors.name: Fed hold + tariff inflation concerns
factors.summary: ABN AMRO expects Fed to remain on hold until December to assess second-round effects from oil shock and tariff-driven goods inflation. No consensus for easing near-term. Longer duration growth stocks (NDX components) remain vulnerable to higher-for-longer real yields.

factors.name: US Dollar strength (DXY near 99.54)
factors.summary: DXY at fresh 7-week highs on Middle East risk premium and geopolitical flight-to-safety. Strong USD headwind for multinational tech earnings (AAPL, GOOGL, MSFT, AMZN derive 40-50% revenue overseas) and weighs on growth multiple expansion.

factors.name: Retail investor positioning rebounding
factors.summary: JPMorgan strategists note retail equity trading share poised to rebound from 4-year lows, potentially providing 'fresh tailwind' for stocks. Suggests latent demand may support near-term momentum if Middle East fears ease.

factors.name: Crypto liquidations & risk-off spillover
factors.summary: Crypto markets shed $80B post-strikes; $935M+ in liquidations wiped out as Bitcoin fell to $72.6K. Tight NQ-BTC correlation means crypto capitulation often precedes broader equity weakness. Tech-heavy Nasdaq historically sensitive to crypto risk-off contagion.

factors.name: Earnings resilience (Hormel, Micron scale-up)
factors.summary: Hormel Foods topped quarterly estimates on strong protein demand; Micron's $1T market cap now backed by actual earnings (per Bloomberg). Suggests underlying corporate health not yet broken despite multiple compression fears.

factors.name: Upcoming US PCE inflation (today, 12:30 GMT)
factors.summary: Core PCE expected to tick up in April per FXStreet preview. Hot surprise would extend real-yield pressure and confirm hawkish Fed posture; cool surprise could ease stagflation fears. Release imminent — high-impact catalyst within hours.

factors.name: Ceasefire deal uncertainty (Hormuz still contested)
factors.summary: Despite hopes for US-Iran peace deal, latest strikes contradict 'deal imminent' narrative. Iranian Foreign Ministry condemned strikes as 'ceasefire violation.' Unresolved geopolitical tail risk keeps options premium elevated and risk assets pressured.

factors.name: Tech mega-cap positioning & gamma sensitivity
factors.summary: Chip/AI rally has drawn extreme short positioning (bearish technicals on fundamentals). NVDA, AAPL, TSLA dealer gamma and options flows likely 2-sided into $30K level; any break above or below could trigger cascade, but binary Middle East outcome dominates.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 rallied to fresh all-time highs earlier in the session on the back of semiconductor strength and retail investor buying interest, but a second round of US airstrikes on Iran near the Strait of Hormuz has triggered a sharp reversal. Oil surged 2.5%+ toward $97/bbl, reigniting stagflation fears and compressing growth multiples. Real yields remain elevated as the Fed signals no near-term cuts (December hold likely per ABN AMRO). While underlying mega-cap earnings (Hormel, Micron) show resilience, the combination of geopolitical uncertainty, hawkish Fed pricing, and extreme short positioning in semis creates a choppy, two-sided market. The imminent US PCE print could provide directional clarity, but the Middle East escalation remains the dominant risk-off catalyst.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE Inflation (April) — 12:30 GMT today; core expected to tick higher; hot print would reaffirm Fed on-hold bias
ECB June Rate Decision expected (June 6); multiple policymakers (Stournaras, Lane, Cipollone) already signaling June hike is 'likely'
FOMC meeting or Fed speakers — monitors for any dovish reversal on rate path given oil shock
US-Iran ceasefire status updates; Hormuz Strait reopening clarity would relieve oil/risk-off pressure
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings guidance — hyperscaler capex commentary on cloud/AI demand remains critical
Treasury yield repricing (10Y, real yields) — watch for any Fed easing signals that could ease duration pressure
screening.risk_warning: Middle East escalation could deepen into broader conflict, spiking oil and volatility beyond technical support levels (sub-29500). Conversely, sudden ceasefire breakthrough or dovish Fed pivot (if PCE surprises cool) would invalidate bearish bias and trigger sharp short-squeeze rally back to $30k+. Micro-cap Russell 2000 weakness despite small-cap outperformance (per Wells Fargo earnings concern) suggests bifurcation risk; if semis roll over on revenue guidance fears, QQQ drawdown could accelerate despite Nasdaq-100 nominal resilience.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (reversion from highs, real-time volatility). Avoid chasing shorts into $29800 support until PCE is released and Middle East headlines stabilize. Consider entry on retest of $30k resistance if PCE cools or ceasefire hope reignites; failing that, wait for stabilization below $29500 before re-engaging longs.
